candidates.evidence_quote: During fiscal 2022, we recorded impairment of assets and lease terminations expense of $31.4 million primarily related to the impairment of long-lived assets for three The Cheesecake Factory, one Other FRC and three Other restaurants.
we recorded impairment of assets and lease terminations expense of $31.4 million
Should the value of goodwill or other intangible assets become impaired, there could be a material adverse effect on our financial performance.
future impairment charges and/or closures may occur if individual restaurant performance does not improve, which could materially adversely affect our financial performance
Our ability to grow our business depends on the availability and selection of high-quality sites that meet our criteria.
the influence of consumer shopping trends on the availability of sites in traditional locations, such as premier shopping centers
we have and may continue to experience longer than usual governmental delays in the licensing and permitting process
the availability and/or cost of raw materials and labor used in construction
the availability of qualified tradespeople in the local market
In recent years, we have experienced an increase in wage and hour litigation, particularly in California.

Significant legal fees and costs in complex class action litigation or an adverse judgment or settlement that are not insured or are in excess of insurance coverage can materially and adversely affect our financial performance.
Several factors may significantly increase our self-insurance costs, such as conditions of the insurance market, the availability of insurance, or changes in applicable regulations.
claims by customers, staff members and others regarding issues such as food-borne illness, food safety, premises liability, dram shop liability
during the past year we learned of a potential compromise of employee credentials that could be used to access our corporate network

Compliance with the California Privacy Laws and other laws relating to the protection of personal information involve significant costs and could result in significant liability in the event we allow an unauthorized disclosure of personal information.

Any failure to maintain financial covenants under the Loan Agreement or to have sufficient liquidity to either repay or refinance the then outstanding balance at expiration of the Loan Agreement, or upon any violation of the covenants, could materially adversely affect our financial performance.

our increased indebtedness related to our acquisition of Fox Restaurant Concepts and our resulting higher debt-to-equity ratio, as compared to that which has existed on a historical basis, could limit our ability to obtain additional financing in the future

If we issue additional shares of our common stock or rights to acquire shares of our common stock, if any of our existing stockholders sells a substantial amount of our common stock, or if the market perceives that such issuances or sales may occur, then the trading price of our common stock may significantly decline.
Hedging activity by investors in the Notes could depress the trading price of our common stock.
Certain provisions in the Notes and the indenture governing the Notes could make a third-party attempt to acquire us more difficult or expensive.
Our failure to pay a dividend or to increase it over time may negatively impact investor confidence in us and may negatively impact our stock price.

The share repurchase program does not have an expiration date, does not require the Company to purchase a specific number of shares and may be modified, suspended or terminated at any time, which may result in a decrease in the trading price of our common stock.
the Inflation Reduction Act of 2022 introduced a 1% excise tax on share repurchases, which increases the costs associated with repurchasing shares of our common stock

Changes in our tax provision or an increase in our tax liabilities, whether due to changes in applicable laws and regulations, the interpretation or application thereof, or a final determination of tax audits or litigation, could materially adversely affect our financial performance.
There can be no assurance that we will be able to timely remediate material weakness in internal controls (if any) or maintain all of the controls necessary to remain in compliance.

Responding to actions of an activist investor may be a significant distraction for our management and staff and could require us to expend significant time and resources, including legal fees and potential proxy solicitation expenses.
changes in financial estimates by research analysts with respect to us or others in the restaurant industry
the equity markets have experienced price and volume fluctuations that affect the stock price of companies in ways that have been unrelated to an individual company’s operating performance
Factors such as comparable restaurant sales that are below our target
When we are unable to achieve sales in a sufficient amount to generate percentage rent obligations, we are not able to fully recoup available allowances at affected sites

A closure of or material damage to one or both of our bakery facilities could impede our ability to supply bakery products to our own and our international licensees’ restaurants as well as to other bakery customers.

notes: Chunk = 1 event only: 10-K 0001104659-23-026072 part 4/6 (Risk Factors continuation + MD&A opening); no fiscal_kpis in this chunk.
All 31 evidence quotes verified verbatim against the event content.

Skipped as vague/duplicative (13): natural-disaster HQ exposure, business-continuity/disaster-recovery boilerplate, adverse construction weather, negative publicity/reputation (secondary effect), PCI compliance (folded into cybersecurity_incident), accounting-standards changes, tax audits (folded into tax_policy), landlord financial viability (folded into restaurant_site_availability), international business growth decline (vague), generic guidance-shortfall risk (metric captured as same_store_sales), M&A announcement volatility (generic), FRC acquisition obligations (company-bound; covered by debt_leverage), COVID-19/macro MD&A section header with no body text in this part.
